mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
mcqs.answer: A. An object in motion will stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law?
mcqs.answer: C. A book resting on a table remains at rest.

mcqs.question: Inertia is related to which of the following?
An object will continue to move at a constant velocity unless acted upon by a ___ force.
What is the term used for the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion?

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by an ___ force.
The property of an object that resists changes in its motion is called ___.
If no net force acts on an object, its velocity will remain ___.
Newton's First Law is also known as the Law of ___.
A stationary object requires a ___ force to start moving.

short_questions.question: What is inertia?
short_questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.

short_questions.question: How does mass affect inertia?
short_questions.answer: The greater the mass of an object, the greater its inertia.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
short_questions.answer: A passenger lurches forward in a bus when it suddenly stops.

short_questions.question: What does an object in motion do if no external force acts on it?
short_questions.answer: It continues to move at a constant velocity.

short_questions.question: What is meant by 'net force'?
short_questions.answer: Net force is the total force acting on an object after all the forces are combined.
